ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR NOTICE OF MOTION FOR AN ORDER TRANSFERRING ACTION TO ORANGE COUNTY HEARING SET FOR OCT-16-2015 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DANIEL ESCAMILLA'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TRANSFERRING ACTION TO ORANGE COUNTY IS GRANTED. THE COURT ORDERS THIS ACTION TO ORANGE COUNTY. THERE IS NO BASIS FOR SAN FRANCISCO VENUE. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO HAVE VENUE IN ALAMEDA COUNTY BASED ON THE CONVENIENCE OF THE WITNESSES IS CURRENTLY IMPROPER. THE COURT AWARDS DEFENDANT ATTORNEY'S FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $500.00 AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNTS OF $450.00 AND $60.00 TO BE PAID BY PLAINTIFFS. DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A REVISED PROPOSED ORDER. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

Notice Of Motion For An Order Transferring Action To Orange County
Off Calendar Oct-16-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Motion For An Order Transferring Action To Orange County (Part 2 of 2) is taken off calendar as a duplicate entry for purposes of entry of tentative ruling only. (302)

Notice Of Motion For An Order Transferring Action To Orange County
Off Calendar Oct-16-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DANIEL CROWLEY, ESQ. of Daniel Crowley & Associates, Tel No. 707-525-8999 specially appearing with and for Defendant DANIEL ECAMILLA;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court received a notice of contest from Plaintiffs' counsel. However, no appearance has been made at today's hearing by or for the Plaintiffs. Argument presented by defense counsel re: Defendant's request for an award of attorney's fees and costs. The Court adopted its tentative ruling with a modification to award attorneys fees and costs, ordering as follows: DEFENDANT DANIEL ESCAMILLA'S Motion For An Order Transferring Action To Orange County is granted. The court orders this action to Orange County. There is no basis for San Francisco venue. Plaintiff correctly notes that a foreign corporation that has failed to register a principal place of business in California may be sued in any county of the state. That rule applies only where all of the defendant/s are unregistered foreign entities, however. See Banta v. Wink, (1897) 119 Cal. 78, 80 ("As defendants Wink and Banta did not reside in this state, they could not move for a change of venue, but that fact did not deprive the other defendants of the right to have the case tried in the county in which they, or some of them, resided at the commencement of the action. It is only when none of the defendants reside in the state that the action may be tried in any county which the plaintiff may designate in his complaint"); Ludington v. La Fortuna Gold, (1906) 4 Cal.App. 369, 370 ("The briefs of the counsel are largely devoted to the proposition of the respondents, that the corporation defendants are nonresidents of the state and may therefore be sued in any county which the plaintiffs may designate. But assuming that this is the case, the right of the plaintiffs to sue them in any county could exist only in a case where they were the only defendants (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 395), and here it appears that there are two other defendants, Neumann and Oliver, both of whom are residents of the state."). Thus, Defendant Escamilla can compel a transfer of venue to his residence in Orange County. See Cubic Corp. v. Sup. Crt. (1986) 186 Cal.App.3d 622, 625. Plaintiff's request to have venue in Alameda County based on the convenience of the witnesses is currently improper. First, plaintiff has not made such a formal motion. Second, it is premature because there is no answer on file. See Easton v.Sup. Crt., (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 245. The Court awards Defendant attorney's fees in the amount of $500.00 and costs in the amounts of $450.00 and $60.00 to be paid by Plaintiffs. DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A REVISED PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
